commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: Hardcoded spacing and radius values (e.g., padding={24}, borderRadius="16px") were used instead of CSS variables from the design system as explicitly requested.
issues.fix: Replace numeric/string literal spacing and radius values with CSS variable references like var(--ggui-spacing-md) or primitive-specific scale keys if the system supports them.

issues.description: Weather emojis lack ARIA roles and labels, making them invisible or confusing to screen reader users.
issues.fix: Wrap emojis in a span with role="img" and provide an aria-label describing the weather condition (e.g., ☀️).

issues.description: The temperature and unit are split into separate Text components and separated by a Box, which may result in disjointed reading by screen readers.
issues.fix: Use a single Text component for the temperature and unit, or wrap them in a container with aria-label combined (e.g., aria-label="28 degrees Celsius").

issues.description: Redundant 'safe' variable assignments. Destructuring defaults (e.g., city = 'Tokyo') already handle undefined/null values for props.
issues.fix: Remove redundant variable assignments like const safeCity = city ?? "Unknown" and use the destructured variables directly.

issues.description: Lack of hover or active states on forecast items which usually suggest drill-down capabilities in weather apps.
issues.fix: Add hover styles (e.g., background change) and transition effects to the forecast Box components to improve perceived interactivity.

issues.description: Icons used for Humidity (menu) and Wind (settings) are not semantically related to the data.
issues.fix: Use more appropriate icons like 'droplet' for humidity and 'wind' for wind speed if available in the library.

issues.description: The 5-day forecast is rendered as a series of Boxes rather than a semantic list.
issues.fix: Wrap the forecast map in a <ul> and each item in a <li> to improve screen reader navigation.

issues.description: Hardcoded date generation inside the component (new Date()) makes the component less predictable/testable.
issues.fix: Pass the current date or formatted date string as a prop.

issues.description: No hover states or interactive feedback on the forecast items.
issues.fix: Add a hover background color or scale transformation to the forecast boxes using CSS variables.

issues.description: The forecast items lack hover or active states, making the component feel completely static.
issues.fix: Add a hover background or scale effect to the forecast Stack items to indicate interactivity or focus.

issues.description: The forecast strip is a sequence of divs/stacks rather than a semantic list, which can be less clear for screen reader users navigating data.
issues.fix: Wrap the forecast mapping in a list structure or use aria-label on the forecast container to identify it as a multi-day forecast.

issues.description: Using array index as key in the forecast map.
issues.fix: Use a unique property like `item.day` for the key if days are unique.

issues.description: Manual override of border-radius on Box components to match Card corners is brittle.
issues.fix: Check if the design system Box primitive supports 'top' or 'bottom' radius props specifically, or use overflow:hidden on the parent Card if supported.

evaluation.critique: The component has excellent layout structure with proper use of Stack/Box primitives and responsive spacing. Design token usage is strong but has minor issues: some hardcoded fallback colors in linear-gradient and inline padding/font-size values (24, 16, 20px) that should use spacing tokens. Polish is adequate but lacks interactive states (hover effects, transitions on forecast items) and loading/error handling.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

issues.description: The prop for updates is named 'taskUpdate' instead of 'onTaskUpdate' as requested in the requirements.
issues.fix: Rename the prop to onTaskUpdate in the interface and usage.

issues.description: Missing the 'edit button' for task titles; editing is only accessible via double-click.
issues.fix: Add a small edit icon button next to the title or in the card header.

issues.description: Double-click to edit is not keyboard accessible or discoverable for assistive technology.
issues.fix: Ensure the edit action is reachable via Tab and Enter, or provide a dedicated visible button with an aria-label.

issues.description: New tasks start in the column where 'Add Task' was clicked, whereas prompt said 'New tasks start in To Do'. However, having it per-column is usually better UX, but technically a requirement mismatch.
issues.fix: Ensure the logic explicitly defaults to 'todo' if that was strictly required regardless of the column clicked.

issues.description: The 'Move task' Select uses a fixed style 'border: none' which might make it hard to identify as an interactive element in some themes.
issues.fix: Use the default Select styling or add a subtle background/border to indicate interactivity.

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: Grid elements are not keyboard accessible. They are implemented as div elements with click handlers but lack focus states or keyboard event listeners.
issues.fix: Change the element container to a 'button' primitive or add tabIndex={0} and a handleKeyDown function (Enter/Space keys) to trigger selection.

issues.description: The periodic table lacks semantic ARIA roles for complex grid layouts.
issues.fix: Add role="grid" to the grid container and role="gridcell" or role="button" to the individual element boxes.

issues.description: Search filtering removes items from the DOM, which can be jarring in a fixed grid layout. While valid, it might be better to visually dim non-matching elements to maintain the table structure.
issues.fix: Instead of filtering the array, map over all elements and apply 'opacity: 0.2' or 'grayscale(1)' to items that don't match the search query.

issues.description: The 'handleAgentData' event listener uses 'any' for the event type.
issues.fix: Define a proper CustomEvent type or use 'Event' and cast the detail property if using a custom event system.

issues.description: Periodic table cells are non-interactive <div> elements with click handlers, making them inaccessible to keyboard and screen reader users.
issues.fix: Replace the Box for elements with a button tag or add tabIndex={0} and an onKeyDown handler for Enter/Space keys.

issues.description: The grid structure lacks semantic ARIA roles for complex data navigation.
issues.fix: Add role="grid" to the container and role="gridcell" to the individual element boxes.

issues.description: Lack of hover state visual feedback on the element cells.
issues.fix: Add a hover transform or background-color shift using CSS or mouse event state management.

issues.description: Color mapping logic is duplicated/verbose within the component body.
issues.fix: Move getCategoryColor and getCategoryBorder to a static configuration object outside the component or use data-attributes with CSS variables for better performance.

issues.description: Conflicting search logic: The component filters elements out of the DOM via useMemo, but also attempts to 'dim' non-matches using opacity. Since non-matches are removed from the array, the dimming logic (opacity: 0.3) is never triggered.
issues.fix: Either keep all elements in the array and use the opacity logic to maintain the table's shape during search (recommended for periodic tables), or remove the unreachable opacity logic.

issues.description: Element buttons have 'outline: none' without a custom :focus-visible replacement, making the table unusable for keyboard-only users.
issues.fix: Remove 'outline: none' or add a high-contrast focus ring style.

issues.description: Interactive elements (grid cells) lack ARIA labels, meaning screen readers will announce disjointed numbers and symbols.
issues.fix: Add aria-label to the button containing the element's full name, symbol, and atomic number.

issues.description: Potential for unstable keys using Math.random() in the element mapping.
issues.fix: Use a combination of index and category or a stable fallback property if atomicNumber is missing.

issues.description: Text inside grid cells can overflow or become unreadable if element names are long, despite the 'truncate' prop.
issues.fix: Set a minimum height for the element name text block or use a conditional font size based on string length.

commit.name: E-Commerce Product Page
commit.description: Product detail page with image, tabs, quantity selector, and cart
commit.prompt:
Build an e-commerce product detail page. Layout:

Top section:
- Product image placeholder (use a colored box with the product name)
- Product title, price (with currency from props), and rating stars (1-5)
- Stock status badge ("In Stock" green or "Low Stock" warning)

Middle section — Tab navigation with 3 tabs:
- "Description" tab: renders product description text from props
- "Specifications" tab: renders key-value pairs from props as a table
- "Reviews" tab: renders a list of reviews (author, rating, text, date) from props

Bottom section:
- Quantity selector (increment/decrement buttons, min 1, max from stock)
- "Add to Cart" button (disabled if out of stock)
- Call props.onAddToCart with { productId, quantity } when clicked

Requirements:
- All product data from props — nothing hardcoded
- Use design system tokens for all colors, spacing, typography
- Price should format with currency symbol from props
- Rating stars should be visual (filled/empty)
- Responsive: stack image above details on mobile widths

- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.description: Product detail page with image, tabs, quantity selector, and cart
commit.prompt:
Build an e-commerce product detail page. Layout:

Top section:
- Product image placeholder (use a colored box with the product name)
- Product title, price (with currency from props), and rating stars (1-5)
- Stock status badge ("In Stock" green or "Low Stock" warning)

Middle section — Tab navigation with 3 tabs:
- "Description" tab: renders product description text from props
- "Specifications" tab: renders key-value pairs from props as a table
- "Reviews" tab: renders a list of reviews (author, rating, text, date) from props

Bottom section:
- Quantity selector (increment/decrement buttons, min 1, max from stock)
- "Add to Cart" button (disabled if out of stock)
- Call props.onAddToCart with { productId, quantity } when clicked

Requirements:
- All product data from props — nothing hardcoded
- Use design system tokens for all colors, spacing, typography
- Price should format with currency symbol from props
- Rating stars should be visual (filled/empty)
- Responsive: stack image above details on mobile widths

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

issues.description: Lack of ARIA roles and labels (e.g., chat log is not marked as role='log', input lacks descriptive label).

issues.description: Hardcoded hex values in inline styles conflict with design system token usage.
issues.fix: Replace hex values with standard theme variables or utilize design system color tokens consistently.

issues.description: Requirement for grouping consecutive messages from the same sender is missing.
issues.fix: Implement a logic block to compare the current message sender with the previous message sender and conditionally omit the sender label/timestamp for consecutive messages.

issues.description: Message list lacks ARIA labels for assistive technologies; user cannot easily discern the role of the message list.
issues.fix: Add role="log" or role="list" to the message container and aria-live="polite" to ensure new messages are announced.

issues.description: The logic for determining if a message belongs to the current user hardcodes 'You' as a sender check alongside the currentUser prop.

issues.description: The sender name and timestamp are present for every single message, causing visual clutter in longer threads.

issues.description: The 'Send' button is missing an aria-label for screen readers when no text is provided (though it is disabled).

issues.description: Using array index as key in the message list mapping can cause re-rendering issues if the list order changes.

issues.description: The 'sender === 'You'' check is redundant if the parent component provides 'currentUser'.
issues.fix: Standardize the check to strictly rely on 'msg.sender === currentUser'.

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding
